primary_factors: No accumulating snow or wintry precip expected (0"; snow probabilities 0) and temperatures stay above freezing through the bus window (roughly 33–36°F, rising after sunrise)
Main operational hazard is dense fog/very low visibility overnight into early morning; could slow buses and elevate crash risk, but Michigan districts rarely fully close for fog alone
Only a localized/conditional slick-spot risk (bridges/overpasses/shaded rural patches) from fog/condensation + marginal temps—not a widespread icing event
Infrastructure signals: roads generally clear; no plow event. Live MDOT/KCRC incident confirmation was not available, increasing uncertainty slightly but not enough to move probability much
News/operations signals: FOX 17 closings page shows no active closings/delays; no Rockford/neighbor district announcements or community “closure buzz”
decision_rationale: All core experts converge on a benign winter-weather setup for Rockford Friday: no snow/ice event is forecast, temperatures remain above freezing through 6–8 AM, and winds are light—so Michigan’s usual closure triggers (accumulating snow, freezing rain/ice accretion, blizzard/drifting, extreme wind chill) are absent. The one meaningful hazard is dense fog (locally near-zero visibility), which can justify slower bus operations and—if widespread and persistent right at bus time—sometimes a 2-hour delay, but full district closure remains historically uncommon. News intelligence strengthens the ‘stay open’ call: no active closings/delays on FOX 17’s system and no Rockford/neighboring-district announcements or community buzz. Power-grid risk is low; Kent County outages appear cleared in the latest check. Given Michigan’s high bar for closures and the lack of a domino-effect signal, the best Brier-optimized forecast is a very low closure probability with a note that delay odds are higher than closure odds if fog verifies as widespread <0.25 mile at 6–7 AM.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 2.0% spread. Average probability: 4%.
